basic_info.title: Metal Gear Solid
basic_info.review: A masterpiece of stealth action that redefined the genre with its cinematic storytelling and innovative gameplay mechanics.

next_game.title: Metal Gear Solid 2: Sons of Liberty

similar_games.title: Metal Gear Solid 2: Sons of Liberty

play_today.details: PC port with widescreen mod support, quality of life improvements, and compatibility fixes for modern Windows systems. Best overall experience.

recommended_patches.name: Widescreen Fix
recommended_patches.description: Enables 16:9 widescreen resolution and removes letterboxing from cutscenes

recommended_patches.name: MGSILauncher
recommended_patches.description: Save file manager, core affinity switching, music restoration patch, controller support fixes

play_today.platform: PlayStation 4 / PlayStation 5 / Xbox Series X/S / Nintendo Switch / Windows (Steam)
play_today.details: Metal Gear Solid: Master Collection Vol. 1 - Official compilation with M2 emulator, multiple regional versions available, includes VR Missions expansion.
Original 1998 PS1 release. Integral version adds VR Missions disc and additional content.

optimal_settings: Internal resolution: 2x-4x for clean retro look or 9x for 4K sharpness
Enable NTSC mode for correct timing
Use texture filtering for smoother visuals

play_today.details: 2004 GameCube remake with updated graphics and gameplay mechanics from MGS2. Includes enhanced cutscenes.
Included in the pre-loaded games of PlayStation Classic console (2018). Limited availability.

The definitive modern experience via the Master Collection Vol. 1. Runs at a stable 60 FPS with unlocked frame rates on PS5, supports modern controllers, and includes cloud saves. Features the enhanced content from the HD Collection, including the new 'Peace Walker' mode and improved textures. While not a full remake, it offers the best balance of convenience, performance, and accessibility for new players.

recommended_patches.name: Metal Gear Solid Master Collection Fix (MGSM2Fix)
recommended_patches.description: A comprehensive mod for the Master Collection on PS4/PS5 that enables custom resolutions, widescreen support, and unlocks modding capabilities for MGS1 and Snake's Revenge.

recommended_patches.name: MGS Master Collection HD Texture Pack
recommended_patches.description: Officially supported texture pack that replaces low-resolution assets in MGS2 and MGS3 within the Master Collection with high-definition versions.

play_today.details: The original 1998 release remains the most authentic experience for purists seeking the exact gameplay and atmosphere intended by Kojima. While it lacks widescreen support and modern QoL features, it is the source material for all remasters. Best played via emulation to bypass hardware limitations, offering a nostalgic journey with the original soundtrack and unaltered mechanics.

recommended_patches.name: MGS1 Widescreen Patch
recommended_patches.description: Enables native widescreen aspect ratio (16:9) and fixes geometry stretching for the original PS1 version when played via emulation.

recommended_patches.name: MGS1 HD Texture Pack
recommended_patches.description: Replaces original low-resolution textures with high-definition versions for the PS1 version, significantly improving visual clarity on modern displays.

recommended_patches.name: MGS1 60 FPS Patch
recommended_patches.description: Modifies the game code to run at a stable 60 FPS instead of the original 30 FPS, providing smoother gameplay for the PS1 version.

optimal_settings: Enable widescreen patch in Patches menu
Set internal resolution to 2x or 4x for sharp retro look
Enable bilinear filtering for smoother sprite scaling
Set frame skipping to 'Auto' for performance optimization
Enable widescreen patch for correct aspect ratio
Set internal resolution to 720p for mobile performance
Configure on-screen controller with custom layout
Enable texture filtering for cleaner visuals

play_today.details: Part of the HD Collection, this version offers a significant upgrade over the original with 720p resolution and 60 FPS gameplay. It includes the 'Peace Walker' mode and improved textures. However, it suffers from occasional frame rate drops and lacks the modern convenience features of the Master Collection. Still a solid choice for those who prefer the PS3 ecosystem.

description.synopsis: Special agent Solid Snake is deployed to Shadow Moses Island to stop a group of terrorists known as FOXHOUND from seizing nuclear warheads. Led by the enigmatic Liquid Snake, the group threatens to launch a nuclear strike. Snake must infiltrate the base, uncover the conspiracy, and prevent a global catastrophe while confronting his own genetic destiny.
key_features: CQC (Close Quarters Combat) system allowing melee takedowns
Stealth mechanics requiring avoidance of enemy line-of-sight
Codec communication system for narrative delivery and intel
Dynamic camera angles and cinematic cutscenes
Cardboard box hiding mechanic for evasion
Multiple endings based on player choices and performance
VR Missions for replayability and skill testing
Real-time strategy elements in managing resources and ammo
Boss battles requiring specific tactical approaches
Integration of film noir and thriller storytelling tropes
legacy_and_impact: Metal Gear Solid revolutionized the stealth genre, proving that video games could deliver complex, cinematic narratives on par with Hollywood films. Its introduction of the Codec system and CQC mechanics became staples in the industry, influencing countless successors. The game cemented Hideo Kojima's reputation as a visionary director and established the Metal Gear franchise as a cultural phenomenon.
